verdict.answer_first: A states the usable recommendation immediately: "Library: `react-i18next`... `next-intl` if you're on Next.js"; B opens with an unasked repo caveat.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly: "Both are mature; pick based on framework"; B also recommends but spends more time qualifying paths.
verdict.fabrication: B invents context: "the app isn't in this directory — `/Users/elon/work/fable-profile`"; A has unsupported estimates like "double the CSS budget" but less invented context.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it gives ordered phases with concrete tasks: "Install i18next... provider... fallback... language switcher".
